Reference: Aurora Operations, Inc. NHTSA-Provided Report # 30530-6818-1
This incident occurred near Ranger, TX on [XXX]  approximately 9:56 am CT on November [XXX], 2023. The Aurora vehicle was operating in autonomy at the time. Conditions were overcast, with light rain. As the Aurora vehicle was driving in its lane at 67 mph, a Ford pick-up truck overtaking the Aurora vehicle in the rightmost lane at approximately 80 mph appears to have hydroplaned, lost control, and hit the passenger-side rear portion of the trailer. 
Before the collision, the Aurora Driver detected the pick-up truck's encroachment into the Aurora vehicle's lane and initiated a nudge to the left to provide space. This was insufficient to completely avoid the collision, and the pickup truck made contact with the passenger side rear of the trailer. The Aurora Driver detected the collision and made the decision to initiate an incident response, but before it could execute that response, the Vehicle Operator took over control of the system and proceeded in manual. The Vehicle Operator received the notification that a collision had been detected and was initiating an incident response. The Vehicle Operator did not visually observe the collision and therefore assumed the reaction of the Aurora Driver to be a software anomaly worth escalated attention, rather than the correct detection of a collision and thus reported the incident through our escalated annotation protocol, rather than through our incident response protocols. We identified the incident at approximately 8:45 am CT on December [XXX], 2023 through a review of our data logs.
At this time, we are not aware of any injuries and, from camera footage, the pick-up truck also appears to have left the scene of the incident. There is also no indication of any cargo or major trailer damage.

This incident occurred near Streetman, TX on [XXX] at approximately 3:03 PM CT on February [XXX], 2024. The Aurora vehicle was operating in autonomy at the time. Conditions were clear. As the Aurora vehicle was driving in its lane at 62 mph, a box truck veered into the lane of the Aurora vehicle. The box truck's mirror appeared to make contact with the back of the trailer that the Aurora Driver was pulling. At this time, we are not aware of any injuries, and camera footage appears to show that the box truck continued to drive. From the post-trip inspection, there was not any visible trailer damage. Before the collision, the Aurora Driver was driving straight in its lane and passing the box truck. Shortly thereafter, the box truck began to encroach into the lane of the Aurora Driver. The Aurora Driver detected the box truck's encroachment into the Aurora vehicle's lane and initiated a nudge away from the box truck to provide space. The Aurora Driver detected the collision and made the decision to initiate an incident response, but before it could execute that response, the Vehicle Operator took over control of the system and proceeded in manual. The Aurora vehicle then re-engaged in autonomy approximately two minutes later. 
The Vehicle Operator did not visually observe the collision, assumed the reaction of the Aurora Driver to be a software anomaly, and reported the vehicle nudge through the standard annotation protocol rather than through the incident response protocols. The incident was identified at approximately 1:03 PM CT on February 2, 2024, through a review of Aurora's data logs.

This incident occurred on March [XXX], 2024 at approximately 13:52 CT near Barstow, TX on [XXX]. The Aurora truck was operating in autonomy at the time. Weather and road conditions were clear. A white passenger car veered into the lane of the Aurora truck and made contact with the left rear of the trailer. The Aurora Driver detected the collision and both the passenger car and the Aurora truck pulled over to the shoulder. Aurora notified law enforcement who arrived on the scene. We have no reports of injuries. The Aurora truck had damage to its rear left impact guard (ICC bar). The white passenger car had damage to its right side front quarter panel. Both vehicles departed the scene.

On February [XXX], 2024 at 1:03 AM PT a Waymo Autonomous Vehicle ("Waymo AV") operating in San Francisco, California was in a collision involving a passenger car on [XXX] at [XXX].
The Waymo AV was stopped in traffic for a red light on [XXX] southbound when a passenger car approached from behind and made contact with the rear of the Waymo AV. Transdev Alternative Services is reporting this crash under Request No. 2 of Standing General Order 2021-01.  This is a duplicate of Report ID 30270-7310-1.

The subject Mercedes-Benz vehicle was driving in the back parking lot of the [XXX] in Santa Clara, CA (parallel to the [XXX]). The Mercedes-Benz vehicle was driven in conventional mode when the driver engaged L3 ADS for a few seconds. The driver then disengaged L3 ADS and continued to drive in conventional mode. The vehicle was manually braked to a stop adjacent to vehicles parked in the parking lot, and perpendicular to a parked Chevrolet Camaro. The driver in the Mercedes-Benz then shifted into Park ("P") via the gearshift lever. While the Mercedes-Benz vehicle was stopped, the Chevrolet Camaro reversed out of its parking space and contacted the Mercedes-Benz vehicle at the right rear door. As a result, the right rear corner of the Chevrolet Camaro and the right rear passenger door of the Mercedes-Benz vehicle sustained minor damage. There were no reported injuries to any of the occupants.

The Mercedes-Benz vehicle was traveling northbound on [XXX] in lane one, near [XXX], with the vehicles L3 ADS system engaged. Traffic ahead began slowing, and the operator attempted a right lane change into lane two. The Mercedes-Benz vehicle operator was unable to complete the lane change and instead applied the brakes. The Mercedes-Benz operator was unable to avoid contact with the Ford vehicle that was in front of it, and the Mercedes-Benz front bumper contacted the rear bumper of the Ford vehicle.

Subject vehicle was on a testing mission in Sunnyvale, CA.  The vehicle was traveling South-bound on [XXX], an eight-lane divided road, operated by the vehicles Level 4 Automated Driving System and supervised by a safety driver in the drivers designated seating position, and with a safety operator in the front passenger seating position.  Subject vehicle moved to the right of the lane to turn right onto West-bound [XXX], a two-lane road, and made contact with a curb at approximately 13-miles per hour.  The safety driver was monitoring the behavior of a vehicle passing in the left lane for signs that it might enter subject vehicles path and did not see subject vehicle moving toward the curb in time to intervene and avoid contact.  During the incident, the safety driver disengaged the Automated Driving System, and then pulled the vehicle to the side of [XXX] and inspected the vehicle and curb for damage.  After the inspection, the safety driver drove the vehicle back to base with the Automated Driving System disengaged.  Subject vehicle did not sustain tire or wheel damage, but the contact resulted in misalignment.  No other agents were involved, no injuries were reported, and law enforcement was not called to the scene. Post-Crash Information: In addition to telematics and video, we also have photographs of the tires, curb, and alignment calibration information.

Subject vehicle was on a testing mission in Mountain View, CA.  The vehicle was traveling North-bound on [XXX] operated by the vehicle's Level 4 Automated Driving System and supervised by a safety driver in the driver's designated seating position, and with a safety operator in the front passenger seating position.  Subject vehicle approached a red light to make a right turn East-bound onto [XXX].  The safety operator suggested that the safety driver disengage the ADS and make the turn in manual mode because the vehicle was hesitant in approaching the intersection. The safety driver disengaged the ADS, and, approximately 250ms later, subject vehicle was lightly struck from behind by a white Mazda3.  Having seen debris on the roadway, the driver and operator believed they had run over a rock and did not realize they were hit by the vehicle. After concluding the mission, the driver and operator found damage to a sensor during an inspection.  A subsequent review of video led to discovery of the crash.  No injuries were reported, and law enforcement was not called to the scene.  
In addition to telematics and video, we also have photographs of the vehicle's damaged sensor.

Subject vehicle was on a testing mission in Emeryville, CA. The vehicle was traveling Westbound on [XXX] operated by the vehicle's Automated Driving System and supervised by a safety driver in the driver's designated seating position, and with a safety operator in the front passenger seating position. Subject vehicle decelerated and came to a complete stop in response to traffic. Approximately three seconds later, with traffic remaining stopped ahead, a white Chevrolet Silverado made contact from the rear. Almost simultaneous with the crash, the safety driver disengaged the Automated Driving System through brake pedal force. After the crash, both vehicles proceeded to the side of the road to exchange information, and then took the closest exit to a nearby parking lot. Subject vehicle was towed given damage to the rear window. No injuries were reported, and law enforcement was not called to the scene.
In addition to telematics and video, we have photographs of the damaged vehicles.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was stopped behind traffic in the left turn lane (lane 2) for a red traffic signal on [XXX] approaching[XXX] in Las Vegas. At that time, an SUV (Vehicle 2) was attempting to merge onto [XXX] from a parking lot on the right when it entered the travel path of a passenger car (Vehicle 3) traveling north on [XXX] in the left through lane (lane 3). The front end of Vehicle 2 struck the front passenger side area of Vehicle 3. Vehicle 3 was redirected to the right after being impacted by Vehicle 2, and it rear-ended the stopped Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy. The Zoox vehicle operator pulled over into a nearby parking lot and returned to the scene to exchange information. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage to the rear bumper and exhaust pipe. Vehicle 2 sustained moderate damage to the front end with an airbag deployment and was towed from the scene. Vehicle 3 sustained major damage to the front end and passenger-side front area with multiple airbag deployments and was towed from the scene. Police and EMS were called to the scene, and the driver of Vehicle 3 was transported by an ambulance. The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report and provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was in the right lane, traveling east on [XXX] in Las Vegas. An SUV (Vehicle 2) to the left of the Zoox vehicle, was traveling ahead in the same direction in the adjacent lane. Just as the Zoox vehicle entered the intersection, the SUV suddenly veered right across the path of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle braked hard in response to the encroaching SUV, the Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy, and shortly after, the front passenger side of the SUV made contact with the front driver side of the Zoox vehicle. The SUV rotated counterclockwise after impact and the rear passenger side of the SUV made contact with the rear driver side of the Zoox vehicle. The SUV sustained major damage with airbag deployment and the Zoox vehicle sustained moderate damage to the driver side. Both vehicles were towed from the scene. The police and EMS arrived at the scene. The Zoox vehicle operator reported "body soreness" and "limping" a few hours after the collision.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was stopped for a red traffic light on westbound [XXX] at the intersection with [XXX] in San Francisco. The SUV driver reversed, then left the scene without attempting to exchange information. After the collision, the Zoox vehicle remained stopped, then later cleared the intersection. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage to the license plate bracket. There were no reported injuries and the police were not called. The Zoox vehicle camera footage shows the SUV driver was not looking ahead at the roadway before or at the time of contact.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was traveling in lane 4 on southwest-bound [XXX] in San Francisco. There were no reported injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was stopped in the left turn lane on westbound [XXX], yielding to cross traffic as it attempted to make an unprotected left onto [XXX] in Las Vegas. Based on hand gestures from a stopped bus waiting to turn in front of the Zoox vehicle, the Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy and proceeded towards making a left turn. Just as the Zoox vehicle started to enter the intersection, Vehicle 2 driver continued straight on [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle operator braked just before the front of the Zoox vehicle made contact with the passenger side fender, front door, and rear door of Vehicle 2. An unoccupied Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped at a red light in the right travel lane on southbound [XXX] at the intersection with [XXX] and [XXX] in San Francisco. At that time, a cyclist on an eBike came to a stop in the bike lane to the right of the Zoox vehicle. The cyclist began to proceed before the light turned green and headed straight. After the light turned green, the Zoox vehicle proceeded straight towards [XXX]. While nearly through the intersection, the cyclist abruptly veered left into the path of the Zoox vehicle and accelerated toward the front of the Zoox vehicle in violation of a red crosswalk signal and in violation of the Zoox vehicle's right-of-way. The Zoox vehicle appropriately braked hard in response to the cyclist's maneuver, but contact was unavoidable. The eBike laterally impacted the Zoox vehicle's right front fender and body side, then the cyclist impacted the right front body of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle then came to a full stop. The cyclist then rode over to the sidewalk and remained briefly stopped on the sidewalk before getting back on the eBike and riding away. The police were not called. No injuries were reported nor apparent with the cyclist.

An unoccupied Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) was traveling in the right lane of eastbound [XXX] near [XXX] in Las Vegas when a passenger car (Vehicle 2) initiated an apparent attempt to make a left turn to cross the eastbound lanes in front of the Zoox vehicle to head west on [XXX]. A Zoox vehicle in manual mode was proceeding with the right of way through a four-way stop, when a person operating an electric scooter failed to stop at the stop sign and proceeded into the intersection from the right and made contact with the rear corner of the Zoox vehicle at low speed. A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was stopped in traffic when a passenger vehicle from behind made contact with the rear of the Zoox vehicle at low speed. The police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was proceeding straight through the intersection of [XXX]. and [XXX]. when it was rear-ended by a passenger vehicle that was switching lanes and made contact with the Zoox vehicle's rear sensors. A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was proceeding westbound with the right of way on a green traffic light at the six way intersection of [XXX], [XXX], and [XXX], when a person operating an electric scooter ran a red light and proceeded northbound into the intersection. A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was stopped on [XXX] facing [XXX], when a passenger vehicle from behind made contact with its rear sensors at low speed. The police were not called and there were no injuries. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle was in autonomy exiting a parking space on [XXX] between [XXX] and [XXX] in San Mateo, CA, with a second vehicle stopped in the lane with its right turn indicator on, when a third vehicle overtook the second vehicle and cut in abruptly from the left, making contact with the front left side and sensors of the Zoox vehicle as it was exiting the space. The Zoox operator disengaged to manual mode just before the contact. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle in manual mode was proceeding straight and in its lane on [XXX] in San Francisco, when an occupant of a parallel parked car abruptly opened a rear door into the side of the Zoox vehicle, causing minor damage to both vehicles. The Zoox vehicle had disengaged for unrelated traffic within 30 seconds of the contact. There were no injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was proceeding straight on [XXX] in San Francisco, Ca when a passenger vehicle turning left from [XXX] on to [XXX] struck the rear of the Zoox vehicle damaging the sensors. There were no injuries and the police were not called. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy had made a right turn from [XXX] onto [XXX] in Menlo Park, CA and was preparing to make an immediate, unprotected left turn onto [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle was slowing and moving at approximately 2 mph with its brake lamps and left turn indicator illuminated, yielding to an oncoming vehicle with the right of way. A following vehicle - traveling at approximately 24 mph - then struck the Zoox vehicle's right rear bumper, and that vehicle's driver airbag appeared to have deployed on impact. The Zoox operator disengaged autonomy, proceeded with the left turn, and pulled over. The following vehicle continued its path of travel, veered out of the lane, and collided with a tree on the left side of the road. The Menlo Park Police Department was called to the scene. Two Zoox operators sustained minor injuries and it is unknown if the driver of the other vehicle sustained injuries. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was proceeding at approximately 2mph towards an exit of a private hotel driveway in Las Vegas, Nevada when it was struck on its right front fender by two individuals involved in an altercation who had darted out from the side of the roadway. The right front fender of the Zoox vehicle was dented. The two individuals fled the scene and Zoox is not aware of any reported injuries. We question whether this interaction meets the reporting requirements nevertheless, we are submitting this report out of an abundance of caution. This 10-Day update provides complete information for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was proceeding straight on [XXX] in San Francisco, CA, in stop-and-go traffic. The Zoox vehicle came to a stop for a vehicle ahead when a following van made light contact with the Zoox vehicle's rear bumper from behind. There were no injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was slowing for a right turn from [XXX] to [XXX] in San Francisco, CA with its turn indicator and brake lights illuminated, when a following bicyclist struck the rear right corner of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle camera bracket was damaged. Police were not called and no injuries were reported. This 10-Day update provides complete information for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle was in autonomy proceeding straight on [XXX] in San Francisco, CA when it encountered a double parked vehicle in lane (vehicle 2). The Zoox vehicle came to a complete stop while waiting for an opportunity to merge around vehicle 2. Vehicle 2 then rolled backwards into the Zoox vehicle, causing minor damage to front sensors and Vehicle 2 rear bumper. There were no injuries and first responders were not notified. Post incident review did not observe any brake lights or reverse lights on vehicle 2 while it was rolling backwards.

A Zoox vehicle was proceeding straight in autonomy through an intersection with a green light and right of way when vehicle 2 attempted an illegal left turn across lanes of traffic into Zooxs right of way. The safety driver disengaged and applied max braking effort which allowed Zoox to mitigate but not completely prevent the unavoidable collision. The Zoox vehicle struck the right front corner of vehicle 2. No injuries were reported and the police were not called to the scene.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was fully stopped with brake lights illuminated in the left turn lane at a red traffic light waiting to complete a left hand turn from [XXX] onto [XXX] in San Francisco, Ca. As the light turned green, the Zoox vehicle appropriately yielded to oncoming traffic when Vehicle 2 attempted to make a lane change to the right and struck the rear of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle incurred minor damage to the rear sensors and bumper. There were no injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped at a red light waiting for traffic ahead on [XXX] with brake lights illuminated. When the traffic light changed to green, surrounding traffic began to move. The Zoox vehicle appropriately continued to wait for a stopped lead vehicle immediately ahead when vehicle 2 struck the Zoox vehicle from the rear. Zoox vehicle camera footage shows the driver of vehicle 2 holding their cellphone raised in their hand at the time of contact. There were no injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was completing a lane change from lane 4 into lane 3 with turn signals illuminated, when Vehicle #2 suddenly swerved from lane 2 into lane 3 making light contact with the left front of the Zoox vehicle. No injuries were reported and police were not called to the scene.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was proceeding straight on [XXX] in San Francisco, CA when a leading vehicle in the lane began to slow down prompting the Zoox vehicle to slow correspondingly. The Zoox vehicle, with brake lights illuminated, had slowed to 5 mph when it was struck from the rear by vehicle 2. Zoox vehicle camera footage shows the driver of vehicle 2 holding their cellphone raised in their right hand. There were no injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped in a merge lane yielding and waiting for cross traffic when a bicyclist approached the side of the vehicle on a parallel path and made a gesture to the driver. When the Zoox vehicle began to move and had reached approximately 5mph, the cyclist changed trajectory towards the Zoox vehicle, which braked, and the cyclist made low speed contact with the rear of the Zoox vehicle. The cyclist reported no injury at the scene, but later followed up with an email stating he was sore. Police were not called, contact information was exchanged, and the cyclist rode away. This 10-Day update provides complete information for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle was proceeding northbound on [XXX]. towards [XXX]. when a passenger vehicle in the right adjacent lane swerved into the Zoox vehicles lane and made contact with the right passenger side door. The other vehicle also made contact with the vehicle in front of them after contacting the Zoox vehicle. There were no injuries reported at that time and police were not called.  Zoox is submitting this updated report to include new information of a claim dated [XXX], 2023, including unspecified "personal injury."  This update provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was proceeding straight in its lane on [XXX] in San Francisco, CA when an occupant of a parallel parked car (Vehicle 2) abruptly opened its rear passenger side door into the side of the Zoox vehicle, causing minor damage to both vehicles. The Zoox operator disengaged as a result of the contact. There were no injuries and the police were not called. This a resubmission of the same report and information submitted February 14, 2023.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was making a right turn to travel southwest onto [XXX] from [XXX] from lane 1 when Vehicle 2 crossed from lane 3 into the Zoox vehicle's lane and made contact with the Zoox vehicle's front sensors before making the right turn. Vehicle 2 did not stop before leaving the scene. No information was exchanged. There were no injuries and the police were not called. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped with brake lights illuminated in traffic southbound on [XXX] towards [XXX]. The other party did not stop to exchange information and left the scene. There were no injuries and police were not called. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was slowing to a stop with brake lights illuminated behind traffic on northbound [XXX] near [XXX] when a vehicle behind made contact at approximately 10mph. There were no injuries and police were not called. Zoox is submitting this updated report to correct a previous reporting error in the HIGHEST INJURY SEVERITY field.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was traveling northbound on an access road towards [XXX] in Las Vegas, Nevada, when the driver disengaged for a vehicle in the oncoming lane rounding a tight curve. Approximately eleven seconds after disengaging, the driver proceeded in manual mode making a right turn and struck the curb at low speed. There were no injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was stopped in a merge lane from northbound [XXX] onto [XXX] yielding and waiting for cross traffic. As it began to creep forward, it again came to a stop and it was struck by the vehicle behind at low speed. There were no injuries and police were not called. This report was updated to change the mileage section to "Highlander".

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was proceeding northbound on [XXX] in stop-and-go traffic through an active construction zone. At the same time, an e-scooter traveling near the right curb was passing other slow traffic behind the Zoox vehicle. Before the e-scooter could also pass the Zoox vehicle on the right, the e-scooter weaved away from the curb, seemingly to avoid a drainage grate, and came within a close following distance behind the Zoox vehicle. As the Zoox vehicle came to a stop again for traffic ahead the e-scooter rider did not stop, and the e-scooter made contact with the right rear tail light of the Zoox vehicle. The e-scooter rider remained upright, did not stop to exchange information, and immediately rode off while the Zoox vehicle was still stopped. There were no injuries and police were not called. The damage to the Zoox vehicle was limited to a cracked tail light lens. This 10-Day update provides complete information for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped in a merge lane, yielding and waiting for cross traffic, when it was rear ended at low speed by a vehicle behind.  A Zoox operator reported "slight shoulder pain." Police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was coming to a gradual stop for traffic ahead when it was rear ended by vehicle 2 at low speed. There was minor damage to the striking vehicle and police were not called. A Zoox operator reported "sore neck and back".

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was slowing to a gradual stop on eastbound [XXX] for a red light at [XXX] when it was rear-ended by vehicle 2 at approximately 10 mph. There was minor damage, no reported injuries, and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped at a stop sign on southbound [XXX] at [XXX] waiting for a crossing vehicle to clear the intersection, when it was rear-ended by an e-scooter rider. The e-scooter rider initially passed the slowing Zoox vehicle while traveling on northbound [XXX], but then made a mid-block U-turn and rode into the rear of the then-stopped Zoox vehicle in a visibly deliberate manner. The contact resulted in minor scratches on the rear bumper of the Zoox vehicle. The e-scooter rider did not initially indicate that they were injured, and the Zoox operator drove through the intersection to a safe pullover location to assess the damage however, the e-scooter rider later indicated that their "foot hurts". The Police and EMS were then called to the scene, and the e-scooter rider apparently received medical attention and was released. This update provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was traveling in the left travel lane of westbound [XXX] between [XXX] and [XXX] while passing a parked wide delivery truck, when a passenger car traveling ahead in the right lane abruptly steered into the Zoox travel lane without using a turn indicator. The Zoox vehicle braked aggressively in autonomy and the safety operator disengaged and conducted additional manual braking, but the collision was unavoidable. The driver of the passenger car left the scene without exchanging information. The Zoox vehicle sustained moderate damage. There were no injuries reported and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle was being driven in manual mode eastbound on [XXX] between [XXX] and [XXX] when contact was made with the left side mirror of a parked car. There was minor damage to the Zoox vehicle's sensor housing and passenger side mirror. There were no injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1)  in autonomy was stopped at a red traffic light on northbound [XXX] at the intersection with [XXX] in San Francisco. At that time, an SUV (Vehicle 2), heading southbound on [XXX], failed to stop for the red traffic light and entered the intersection, causing a collision with a passenger car (Vehicle 3) that was traveling eastbound on [XXX]. The police and EMS personnel were called to the scene. The driver of the SUV left the scene without exchanging information. The passenger car appeared to sustain significant damage to the front end. There were no reported injuries.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was traveling on southbound [XXX] near [XXX] in San Francisco. The Zoox vehicle slowed to a gradual stop with brake and hazard lights activated during a simulated pick up-drop off maneuver when it was rear-ended by a minivan (Vehicle 2). The rear bumper of the Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage. There were no reported injuries, and police were not called. Zoox vehicle camera footage shows that the driver of the minivan may not have been looking ahead at the roadway prior to and at the time of contact.

A Zoox vehicle (vehicle 1) in autonomy was stopped at a red traffic light on southbound [XXX] at [XXX] when it was rear-ended at low speed by a passenger car (vehicle 2). There was minor damage to the Zoox vehicle. There were no reported injuries and police were not called. Zoox vehicle camera footage shows that the driver of the passenger car may not have been looking ahead at the roadway prior to and at the time of contact.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomy was stopped, yielding to cross traffic as it attempted to turn right from westbound [XXX] onto northbound [XXX]. The safety operator disengaged and continued to brake, when the Zoox vehicle was struck by the vehicle behind. The police were not called. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage and the safety operator reported back pain a few hours after the collision.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was traveling southwest on [XXX] and turning right onto [XXX] on a green traffic signal. The rear bumper cover and rear radar unit on the Zoox vehicle sustained damage. The hood, front grille and front bumper cover on the passenger car appeared to have sustained damage. There were no reported injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy, traveling northeast on [XXX], was stopped in lane for a red traffic light at [XXX]. A SamTrans bus (Vehicle 2), with a front-mounted triple-bike rack extending past the width of the bus on the driver's side, was traveling southeast on [XXX] and attempting a right turn onto [XXX]. The protruding edge of the bike rack was encroaching into the Zoox vehicle's travel lane as the bus was executing the right hand turn. As the light turned green the Zoox vehicle inched forward and attempted to steer away from the bus. Just as the safety operator disengaged from autonomy, the protruding edge of the bike rack made contact with the driver's side fender on the Zoox vehicle. There were no reported injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was traveling east on [XXX] in stop and go traffic in the right travel lane, approaching the intersection with [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle slowed gradually for a stopped vehicle ahead in the same travel lane, when it was rear ended by an SUV (Vehicle 2) that was traveling directly behind. The Zoox vehicle sustained moderate damage and it appears that the SUV may have sustained moderate damage as well. There were no reported injuries. The Police and EMS were then called to the scene, however, it was reported that only the EMS personnel arrived at the scene.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1), operating in manual mode, was traveling northeast on [XXX] in the right travel lane, approaching the intersection with [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle slowed for slowing traffic ahead in the same travel lane, when it was rear ended by a pickup truck (Vehicle 2) that was traveling directly behind. The Zoox vehicle sustained moderate damage and the safety operator reported neck pain after the collision. The police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was traveling west on [XXX] and turning right onto [XXX]. While executing a low-speed turn, the Zoox vehicle braked and then the operator disengaged. As the Zoox vehicle was slowing, the motorcycle (Vehicle 2) operator accelerated in an apparent attempt to overtake the Zoox vehicle. The motorcycle then struck the driver's side rear bumper and quarter panel of the Zoox vehicle and the motorcycle and the operator fell to the ground. The motorcycle sustained scrapes and the Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage to the rear bumper and trim. Both parties exchanged information. The police were not called. The motorcycle operator did not report any injuries and rode their motorcycle from the scene. Subsequently, the Zoox vehicle operator reported lower back pain/tightness.
The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report and provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was stopped at a red light in the right travel lane on southbound [XXX] at the intersection with [XXX]/[XXX] in San Francisco. After the light turned green, the Zoox vehicle entered the intersection and began bearing right onto southbound [XXX] when a lane-splitting motorcycle (Vehicle 2) operator maneuvered around other traffic and quickly came up on the rear of the Zoox vehicle, traversing laterally in lane from the left to the right side of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle braked and then the Zoox vehicle operator disengaged. The motorcycle then struck the passenger's side of the rear bumper and quarter panel on the Zoox vehicle and the motorcycle and its operator fell to the ground. The motorcycle operator appeared to have scraped his hands, but declined medical attention. The motorcycle sustained minor damage to the fairing, left side mirror, and front and rear left turn signal lens and the Zoox vehicle did not sustain damage. The motorcycle operator left the scene without exchanging their information. The police were not called.
The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report and provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was in the process of making a right turn at a traffic light from westbound [XXX] onto [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle had advanced and then came to a stop as it yielded to cross traffic. Just when the Zoox vehicle began to move again it was rear ended by a passenger car (Vehicle 2) behind. The safety operator disengaged after the collision. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage. There were no reported injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was traveling westbound on [XXX] in [XXX] when a parallel-parked passenger car (Vehicle 2) abruptly entered the Zoox vehicle's travel lane from a parking space in the right parking lane without yielding or activating a turn signal. Vehicle 2 made contact with the right front side and wheel of the Zoox vehicle, and the Zoox vehicle made contact with the driver's side door and left front tire/wheel well of the passenger car. Both vehicles sustained moderate damage and the Zoox vehicle was towed from the scene. There were no reported injuries at that time and police were not called. Zoox is submitting this updated report to include new information on an unspecified "soft tissue injury" claim received on December 3, 2024, from one of the passengers in Vehicle 2.  This update provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1), was completing a mission traveling southwest on [XXX] in San Francisco. As the vehicle was completing its mission and slowed to a near stop, the Zoox vehicle operator identified an oncoming bicycle, disengaged from autonomy, and braked the Zoox vehicle to a full stop in its lane. The bicyclist crossed into the oncoming traffic lane occupied by the Zoox vehicle and continued rapidly while steering directly towards the front of the Zoox vehicle. The bicycle made contact with the front bumper of the stationary Zoox vehicle and the bicyclist made contact with the hood of the Zoox vehicle and fell to the ground. The police and emergency responders arrived at the scene and the bicyclist was transported to a hospital. The police report describes injuries to the bicyclist as "loss of consciousness, complaint of pain to back, knee, and toe nail". This 10-Day update provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was gradually slowing to a stop in the right travel lane (lane 5) for a red traffic signal on southbound [XXX] at the intersection with [XXX] in Spring Valley, Nevada. A motorcyclist (Vehicle 2), also southbound on [XXX], was traveling at high speed in the left through lane (lane 3), aggressively approaching a slowing SUV (Vehicle 3) in lane 3. The motorcyclist clipped the right rear corner of Vehicle 3, deflecting the motorcycle to the right, causing the motorcyclist to lose control and fall to the pavement. The motorcyclist was sliding on the pavement when the motorcycle made contact with the Zoox vehicle's rear bumper. The Zoox vehicle operator disengaged from autonomy and brought the vehicle to a stop. The motorcycle came to rest on its right side with the motorcyclist's right leg stuck underneath the motorcycle. An adjacent bystander immediately aided the motorcyclist and asked the Zoox vehicle operator to move the Zoox vehicle "slightly forward". After the Zoox operator moved the vehicle forward, the bystander lifted the motorcycle and the motorcyclist remained on the ground for a short period of time before standing up and walking around the scene. The motorcyclist was observed to have injuries. Police and EMS were called to the scene and the motorcyclist was later transported by an ambulance. The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report and provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was stopped at a stop sign waiting for traffic to clear to make a left turn from northeast bound [XXX] onto [XXX] in San Francisco. A passenger car (Vehicle 2) was stopped directly behind the Zoox vehicle apparently in the process of turning right onto [XXX]. As the Zoox vehicle advanced and then came to a stop as it yielded to cross traffic, the passenger car tried to pass the Zoox vehicle on the right side and contacted the Zoox vehicle. The front bumper on the passenger car and rear bumper on the Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage. There were no reported injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was traveling west on [XXX] in the left travel lane, slowing as it approached the intersection with [XXX] in San Francisco. The Zoox vehicle operator disengaged from autonomy and braked further for stopped traffic ahead in the same travel lane. At that time, a Tesla sedan (Vehicle 2) traveling directly behind the Zoox vehicle abruptly attempted a lane change maneuver into the adjacent lane on the right,  side-swiping a BMW sedan (Vehicle 3) that was also westbound on [XXX] in the right lane. Vehicle 2, after making contact with Vehicle 3, veered back to the left lane behind the Zoox vehicle and rear ended the stopped Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage, while Vehicle 2 and Vehicle 3 appeared to have sustained moderate damage. There were no reported injuries and the police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was traveling west on [XXX] in the right turn lane towards [XXX] in San Francisco, California. The Zoox vehicle was driving around some in-lane debris when a passenger car (Vehicle 2), attempting to enter traffic from a parking lot on the right, entered the Zoox vehicle's travel path. The front bumper of the Zoox vehicle on the passenger side made contact with the driver's side fender and front bumper of the passenger car. There were no reported injuries and police were not called. Zoox was later notified of a claim for unspecified damages by the other involved party.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was traveling in the right lane on northbound [XXX] towards [XXX], beginning to veer right as the lane split into a right turn lane.  An SUV ( Vehicle 2), traveling ahead in the right lane was braking for slowing traffic straight ahead when it abruptly steered right into the Zoox vehicle's travel path. The Zoox vehicle braked and its operator then disengaged from autonomy and continued braking. The front passenger side of Vehicle 2 struck the front driver side of the Zoox vehicle. Both vehicles sustained damage - the Zoox vehicle was towed from the scene while the operator of Vehicle 2 drove their vehicle from the scene. The police were called but did not arrive at the scene. There were no reported injuries.

A Zoox vehicle in autonomous mode was stopped for a red traffic light on northbound [XXX] while preparing to turn left onto westbound [XXX] in San Francisco. After the light turned green the Zoox vehicle began to move forward and then slowed gradually to yield to a pedestrian with right of way in the crosswalk on [XXX]. Just as the Zoox vehicle slowed to almost a stop (0.2 mph), a skateboarder quickly approached the rear of the Zoox vehicle extending their left arm forward and making contact with the right rear corner of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy and brought the vehicle to a stop. The skateboarder initially told the Zoox vehicle operator they were not injured but later mentioned they "smacked" their hand and it was "bruised." When asked, however, they declined medical attention. The Zoox vehicle did not sustain damage. The police were not called. The Zoox vehicle camera footage shows that the skateboarder was looking at their cell phone and did not appear to have been looking ahead at the roadway before the time of contact.The content of this report is unchanged from the initial report and provides complete information to date for this incident.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was stopped in stop and go traffic in lane 3 of northbound [XXX] in Paradise, Nevada. While the Zoox vehicle was stopped, an SUV (Vehicle 2), also in lane 3 of northbound [XXX], moved into lane 2. As the SUV passed the Zoox vehicle, the passenger side mirror of the SUV tapped the driver side mirror of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy. The cover of the driver side mirror on the Zoox vehicle detached from the housing and there was no damage to the other vehicle. There were no reported injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was in the right travel lane, traveling east on [XXX] approaching the intersection with [XXX] in Paradise, Nevada. An SUV (Vehicle 2) was traveling in the same direction, in the adjacent lane to the left of the Zoox vehicle. Shortly after the two vehicles cleared the intersection with [XXX], the SUV veered right into Zoox vehicle's travel lane. The passenger side fender of the SUV made contact with the driver side quarter panel of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy. There was minor damage to the Zoox vehicle. The driver of the other vehicle did not stop and left the scene without exchanging information. There were no reported injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) operating in manual mode was stopped behind traffic for a red traffic light on southbound [XXX], approaching the intersection with [XXX] in San Francisco. After the traffic light turned green, and just as the Zoox vehicle started to move (0.2 mph), it was rear ended by an approaching minivan (Vehicle 2). The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage to the rear bumper. There were no reported injuries and police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was in the process of making a right turn at a traffic light from westbound [XXX] onto [XXX] in Spring Valley, NV. The Zoox vehicle advanced and then gradually slowed to a stop as it yielded to cross traffic. The Zoox vehicle then advanced and slowed gradually to a stop, again, to yield to cross traffic when it was rear-ended by a passenger car (Vehicle 2) behind. The safety operator disengaged after the collision. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage and the passenger car appeared to have also sustained minor damage. Both parties exchanged information. The police were not called.  Subsequently, the Zoox vehicle operator reported back pain.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomy was stopped at a red light behind an SUV (Vehicle 2) in the right travel lane on [XXX] at the intersection with [XXX] and [XXX] in San Francisco. After the light turned green, the SUV proceeded straight, in the right lane, toward southbound [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle also proceeded and transitioned into the left lane (also a through lane). While almost through the intersection, the SUV suddenly veered left across the path of the Zoox vehicle toward [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle braked hard in response to the turning SUV and had almost reached a stop (0.5 mph) when the rear driver side of the SUV made contact with the passenger side front fender of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle then came to a full stop. The driver of the SUV left the scene without exchanging information. The Zoox vehicle cleared the intersection, proceeded down [XXX], briefly stopped again, and then pulled over on [XXX]. The Zoox vehicle sustained minor damage to the passenger side front fender. The police were not called.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was traveling in the right lane on southbound [XXX] approaching [XXX] in Paradise, NV. The Zoox vehicle operator disengaged from autonomy and drove over an in-lane, dislodged manhole cover with the front passenger side tire of the vehicle, causing the manhole cover to rise and strike the passenger side rear wheel and tire. The impact debeaded the tire and fractured the wheel rim. The Zoox vehicle then rotated clockwise, towards the concrete planter on the right side abutting the curb. The Zoox vehicle operator then braked and the front end of the Zoox vehicle made contact with the concrete planter. Besides damage to the passenger side wheel and rim, the Zoox vehicle's front end also sustained damage. The police were called to the scene and there were no reported injuries.

A Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) in autonomous mode was in the right lane, traveling west on [XXX] towards [XXX] in Paradise, Nevada. An SUV (Vehicle 2)  to the left of the Zoox vehicle, traveling ahead in the same direction in the adjacent lane, suddenly veered right into the path of the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle braked in response to the encroaching SUV when the front passenger side of the SUV made contact with the driver's side front and rear doors on the Zoox vehicle. The Zoox vehicle operator then disengaged from autonomy. Both vehicles proceeded a few feet further and stopped briefly in the right lane. Both vehicles then pulled over into a nearby parking lot to exchange information. Both vehicles sustained moderate damage and the Zoox vehicle was towed. The subject vehicle began its testing drive from the Embassy Suites parking lot with the subject vehicle engaged in autonomous mode.  Two operators were seated in the front seats of the subject vehicle.  At the start of the route the operators confirmed that all the ADS hardware and software were working properly. The subject vehicle drove south on [XXX] toward [XXX] and then turned right on [XXX].  After making the right turn onto [XXX], the operators of the subject vehicle noticed a stopped car (the Honda Pilot) on the stop line of the exit of a parking area of a retail center waiting to enter onto [XXX]. The subject vehicle was driving in the far right lane with the flow of traffic at approximately 30km/h (18mph). As the subject vehicle passed in front of the Honda Pilot, the Honda Pilot began to exit the parking lot without yielding. The Honda Pilot's front bumper collided with the right rear part of the subject vehicle, causing damage to the right rear body and rear right wheel of the subject vehicle. The damage to the right rear wheel of the subject vehicle required tow truck assistance.  The Honda Pilot sustained damage to its front bumper but did not require tow truck assistance.  No persons have reported injuries.  No other vehicles or other property were involved in the incident.  On the scene of the incident, the driver of the Honda Pilot admitted fault for the accident to the subject vehicle's operators.

On May [XXX], 2025, a Plus autonomous test truck was driving in autonomous mode on a public highway in Texas with a Safety Driver.  Plus's safety drivers are experienced and well-trained. The Safety Driver operating the particular vehicle has 12-years of over-the-road commercial vehicle driving experience. All safety drivers are trained to manage potential hazards they may encounter on the roads and are encouraged to retake control of the vehicle whenever necessary to ensure safe operation.  
Here, the Plus vehicle was traveling Northbound on [XXX] at approximately 63 mph in mild to moderate traffic with clear weather conditions.  According to the video camera log, the truck was in autonomous mode when a black SUV traveling in the right-most driving lane swerved to the right into the emergency lane (i.e., the shoulder) to avoid a vehicle that had stopped before it in the driving lane. Seeing the disabled vehicle in the driving lane, the Plus Safety Driver took control of the truck by applying the brakes and began to decelerate the vehicle. Two vehicles traveling behind the black SUV in the same lane then swerved to the left and into the lane in which the Plus vehicle was traveling.  The left side of the second of these vehicles (a minivan) made contact with the Plus truck's right side. [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION]
The Plus truck swerved slightly into the lane to its left as a result of the contact, avoiding a pickup truck that was passing by it. The Safety Driver then re-centered the vehicle into the truck's original lane. After the contact, the minivan moved into the rightmost lane and then ultimately came to a stop in the emergency lane.  The Plus Safety Driver changed lanes and also brought the truck to a stop in the emergency lane.
A Police report was taken at the scene, but the Plus Safety Driver did not receive a copy of it.  Based on our best information, none of the passengers in the minivan reported injuries and the only damage was to the vehicle's driver-side tire.  The minivan nevertheless was towed away from the scene.  The Plus truck did not have any damage and was able to continue driving to its destination.

A Local Motors electric Olli shuttle (V1.0) (VIN Number [XXX]), equipped with the Robotic
Research autonomy kit (enabling autonomous steering, acceleration,
deceleration, and braking (AutoDrive)), was proceeding forward in autonomous
mode through a parking lot in Yellowstone National Park. A parked passenger
vehicle began reversing into the path of the Olli without signaling or yielding the right of way to
oncoming shuttle. The autonomy system detected the encroaching vehicle and commanded a braking
event, which began deceleration of the Olli. Though the Olli was nearly stopped
by the time the of the accident, a collision still occurred, causing damage
to the right rear of the passenger vehicle. The safety operator inside the
Olli also pulled the handbrake when they became aware of the oncoming
collision, but this was not performed in time to avoid the collision. For
reference this incident is under review with the Box-7 Team at NHTSA.

On January [XXX], 2023, RRAI received a support request through their Customer Support Portal from Beep, Inc., the owner of the vehicle, regarding their Olli 2.0 vehicle bumping into the curb in Peachtree Corners, GA. At 11:01 am, while running the normal route, the Olli contacted the adjacent curb on the front right side, causing tie rod and cosmetic damage. No one was injured and the only damage was to the vehicle itself. The customer contacted RRAI through the client support portal shortly after the incident. No injuries occurred, there were no passengers besides the safety operator, no other vehicles were involved, and no emergency systems were engaged.
Olli 2.0 (S/N 2-18) is an 8-passenger shuttle that is no longer manufactured. The vehicle manufacturer, Local Motors, dissolved in 2022. Local Motors contracted RRAI to install autonomy kits for several Olli shuttles during the period of 2018 - 2020. RRAI provided some support for initial site deployment and ensured customer safety operators were trained on the autonomy system. Since the Local Motors dissolution in 2022, almost all Olli vehicles have been decommissioned.
To date, we have concluded the following:
1. There were no updates or alterations made by RRAI in the months prior to Jan [XXX]. In previous operations, including the immediately preceding operations on January 20th, 2023, Olli 2-18 ran the route as normal with no anomalies. There were no software updates or other system changes before it was run again on January [XXX], 2023, the date of curb contact.
2. The root cause of the curb contact was a communication error with the HD Map registration module. HD Map registration utilizes real-time lidar and positioning data and compares that to a-priori HD maps stored within the autonomy system. A communication problem occurred between the computer running the map registration software and the computer running the navigation algorithms. The cause for the communication problem has been identified and a fix has been implemented to prevent the issue from happening again (see below).
3. Steering was out of calibration. It was noted through data collection that the steering was out of calibration at time of incident. The autonomy system was attempting to correct this issue, but the vehicular steering system could not achieve the required turn radius. This is a vehicular maintenance concern managed by the customer.
It was determined that the HD map registration issue had not been observed during autonomous operations in the previous four years. The solution to prevent further occurrences includes:
1. Implementing a self-diagnostic routine upon system boot, which ensures the communication connection is established. If the connection is not established, this diagnostic will prevent the vehicle from driving autonomously. 
2. Updating the error checks to ensure that during run time, the vehicle will come to a controlled stop in event of a communication problem.  
3. Working with the customer to recalibrate the steering, and assisting in reviewing customer's vehicle maintenance checks for steering calibration if necessary.
4. Continuing to review data to determine other concerns or improvements.

The vehicle in front of the customer's vehicle slowed down abruptly on the freeway and the customer immediately stepped on the brake to slow her vehicle down. The customer's vehicle slowed down in a straight line without locking-up of the wheels, indicating ABS worked as intended. The customer's vehicle was then rear-ended by the vehicle which was unable to stop resulting in a minor fender-bender on the freeway. The customer was traveling on a two lane, country road, which was missing lane markings on the sides of the road - only the middle line was properly marked. The speed limit on the country road is 35MPH. The customer noticed a pothole on the side of the road, swerved towards the middle of the road to avoid it, but the LKA assist engaged and moved the vehicle back in the lane. By doing so, the vehicle ran over the pothole, with both front and back passenger side tires and damaged both wheel and tires in the process. During our investigation, we determined that in this case the LKA worked as intended. The system is designed to prioritize a solid line, and can't detect a pothole, and since the road is very narrow (our estimate is less than 3m per side) the vehicle unfortunately ran over the pothole. LKA/ELK system limitations throughout the industry are similar: the LKA/ELK systems try to keep vehicle inside the lane, to prevent a vehicle from drifting out, and the system can't predict driver intention, for example: Is the driver crossing solid lane intentionally or by accident, is hard to distinguish. Relevant override conditions in this case are:
- Steering wheel speed  200deg/s
- Steering torque  3.5Nm
In our application, the override force is limited by the EU homologation test and therefore, we feel that the vehicle behaved as expected and should have been over-powered by the driver to avoid the pothole, thus eliminating a costly replacement cost of two wheels and tires.

On September [XXX], 2025, the customer called Kias Customer Care Center regarding his 2025MY Kia K4, indicating that, in July he was in a car accident and the lane assist  jerked to the left and hit a barrier. The customer reported that before this incident, he had noticed it would start to jerk the wheel, but he always had hands on [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION], so [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION] could correct it easily. However, on this occasion, the wheel jerked to the left and [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION] tried to correct it, but it was semi-locked and wouldnt turn, resulting in the vehicle crashing into a cable barrier. The customer alleged his left shoulder hurt.. The customer requested an inspection of the vehicle.
The subject Kia K4 is equipped with Lane Following Assist (LFA), Lane Keeping Assist (LKA), and Smart Cruise Control (SCC). It is unclear which feature the customer is referring to by lane assist. However, only LFA and SCC engaged at the same could qualify as a Level 2 ADAS feature under the proper conditions.
LFA is a supplemental system that helps to detect lane markings and/or vehicles on the road and assists the driver's steering to help keep the vehicle between lanes. The functionality of LFA is contingent upon several outside factors and it is always the responsibility of the driver to safely steer the vehicle and to maintain the vehicle in its lane. 
Kia has attempted to contact the customer on several occasions to obtain additional information to evaluate the incident but customer has not responded.

The [XXX] was driving on [XXX] towards [XXX] in Riverside California. A minivan reversed out of a parking position (perpendicular parking to road lane) from the right side of the road into the shuttle¢ÂÂs path and struck the shuttle from the side.
After the incident a supervisor from the Riverside Transport Agency RTA (operator or the vehicle) was called to the side. The RTA supervisor recorded the incident according to RTAs protocols.
In addition to the [XXX] trainer/supervisor being on the vehicle additional [XXX] personal was called to the side as well.

Summary of Events:
-Upon detecting the minivan in the side envelope, the system issued a brake command within 5ms.
-Brake pressure began rising 58ms after the command and exceeded 90% of the requested pressure 164ms after the initial command.
-The minivan reversed out of a parking space perpendicular to the [XXX] Ã¢ÂÂs direction of travel and struck the Lift on the side.
-The minivan continued reversing until contact occurred.
-The elapsed time from first detection of the minivan in the side envelope to impact was approximately 358ms.
-The vehicle came to a complete stop in 1.178 seconds.

In Summary:
The other car pushing out from the parking position did not give way as would have been required.
The [XXX] detected the other car getting into the right side of its safety envelope just 358ms before the impact. The reaction time of the [XXX] was as expected in terms of reacting to the signal and initiating a brake maneuver. However, the short time and distance between the other car entering the [XXX] safety envelope (and consequently its track) was too short to avoid the collision. Even if the [XXX] had come to a complete stop before the actual impact the collision would most likely have happened but potentially at a slightly different position.

**1-Day Incident Report 5/25/2024**
Customer contacted Kia Customer Care (KCC) to report an incident involving his 2023MY Kia Sportage. Specifically, the customer called to thank Kia "for saving [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION] life." Customer explained he was in a head-on collision when a drunk driver suddenly veered into his lane.  Customer reported he suffered "cuts, bumps & bruises" and stated he "believes that [REDACTED, MAY CONTAIN CONFIDENTIAL BUSINESS INFORMATION] lack of injuries is due to the plethora of safety features my Sportage had."  KCC subsequently contacted the customer to obtain additional details regarding the incident.  Customer stated that he was driving on a two-lane highway around 3AM, with cruise control engaged, when the car he was following veered into a ditch and a drunk driver veered into his lane. Customer did not see the drunk driver until the last second and as a result did not have time react.  When asked by KCC, the customer responded that Highway Driving Assist (HDA) was not engaged, but that his Smart Cruise Control and Lane Following Assist were engaged at the time of the incident.  The customer reported that the Sportage's airbags deployed, both the customer and driver of the other vehicle (unclear if the drunk driver or the driver of the vehicle that veered into the ditch) were transported to the hospital and that the Sportage was towed. Customer stated he was not asking anything from Kia and that he had called only to share his appreciation. 
This incident is being reported as precautionary measure since the incident meets the requirements under the SGO.  However, based on the description of the incident provided by the customer, the incident was not caused by a failure and/or disengagement of smart cruise control and/or LFA.

The driver of the truck did not pull over after the collision.
